instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Five Wealth Creation Strategies to Invest Successfully
As any seasoned investor would know, the path to successful wealth creation is a rocky one. Sure, there are some smooth passages. However, if you want to create a considerable amount of wealth via an investment plan in the long term, then you are likely to face many ups and downs.
The last 2-3 years have seen the markets experience a high range of volatility as the pandemic surged and gradually declined. For veteran investors, this may not stray them off the investment path, as they have probably experienced such patterns over the years. For new investors, however, the constant market volatility can make investing, especially in the equity market, seem like an intimidating avenue.
Following some time-tested strategies can be of immense help in such a situation. With that in mind, here’s a look at five wealth-creation strategies that can help make your investment plan a success.
1. Consider the business value of the stock/s
Essentially, this strategy states that you look at the underlying workings of a business rather than just calculating whether the stock of that business is likely to go up in the short term.
This strategy has been promoted largely by the legendary investor, Warren Buffet, who believes that one should “write down exactly why they plan to invest in a particular company” when making an investment plan. Some vital pointers Mr. Buffet advises considering when penning down the ‘why’ include:
The returns earned by the company on its net capital (should be good)
The functioning of the managerial teams (should be competent and honest)
The pricing of the stocks (should be sensible and practical)
This can help you choose stocks of businesses that are likely to make good and stable returns in the long term, even when the economic scenario is not that positive.
2. Invest for the long term
If you are investing in the market, then do it with a long-term approach, is the advice of investment experts. This strategy is ideal for investors, especially if you are investing via an insurance plan, such as a ULIP (Unit-Linked Insurance Plan).
The longer you invest, the larger the benefits of compounding you can receive. For the unaware, compounding works by allowing you to gain interest on the previously earned interest along with the principal amount. Thus, the more time you give your investment to grow, the higher will be the overall compounding interest earned in those years.
Besides, a long-term insurance plan also ensures that you receive a life cover for a longer period.
3. Diversify across different aspects
When determining your investment plan, it is important to diversify your money across various aspects, such as sectors, market cap, the risk spectrum, and so on. This is different from diversifying your investment across just stocks, which may or may not belong to the same industry or risk category.
Following the multiple diversification strategy decreases the likelihood of losses even during market volatility, since you may have some stocks in your kitty that may be largely unaffected by the same.
4. Start early, even with low amounts
As they say, something is better than nothing. Investing early with a low amount is always preferable to not investing at all. Many people, especially the youth, shy away from making investments because they believe that they need to start with a large amount.
Compounding works ideal if one focuses on consistency rather than the intensity of the investment. A small amount invested on a regular basis in a stable investment plan can lead to a huge corpus in many years’ time.
Consider two investors - A and B.
While A began investing Rs 12000 every year from the age of 22, B began investing Rs 48,000 every year from the age of 35. At the age of 50 years, A’s investment had grown to Rs 27.5 lakhs. When B reached 50 years of age, his investment had grown to Rs 20 lakhs only. *
*This figure is arrived at by assuming an estimate of 12% CAGR.
5. Take the help of experts
Today, a newbie investor has a variety of resources at their disposal, be it nifty tools, such as the investment calculator, or free tutorials on investment basics. This does not mean, however, that experts have lost their demand or value.
To make your investment plan more effective, it is always recommended to consult financial experts. When you invest via a ULIP, you have the benefit of consulting the fund/investment managers and experts that deal with your money and help make it grow. Thus, there is a sense of assurance that your money is in the right hands.
Conclusion
It is quite natural to get confounded by the ever-changing barrage of investment advice. However, one must remember that investment, in its fundamentals, is quite a simple practice. One’s financial journey, whether it is via an insurance plan or retail investing, becomes much easier as a result.
Reference/s:
https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/markets/stocks/news/road-to-wealth-creation-5-effective-strategies-to-become-a-successful-investor/articleshow/94262850.cms?from=mdr]
input.compliance_comments:
[Drustikumari Barot]: https://www.cnbc.com/2020/02/24/warren-buffett-dont-buy-stocks-because-you-think-they-will-perform.html (Context: This strategy has been promoted largely by the legendary investor, Warren Buffet...)
[Drustikumari Barot]: https://groww.in/calculators/sip-calculator] (Context: *This figure is arrived at by assuming an estimate of 12% CAGR.)
